title: Memecylon centrale
taxonomy: Memecylon centrale belongs to the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the memecylid or tarrietia family. This family is native to tropical regions of the world, particularly in Africa and Asia. Memecylon centrale is a species within this family and is likely classified as a shrub or small tree. Further classification of Memecylon centrale would involve its genus and species levels, but these are generally within the family Melastomataceae. The classification of Memecylon centrale would be based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.
uses: Memecylon centrale, a plant species, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and other parts are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The plant is also reported to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Additionally, it is used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. Further research is necessary to fully understand the extent of its medicinal applications.

preparations: Memecylon centrale herbal preparations are used to treat various ailments, with a focus on respiratory issues.
